meanings.definition: cause to sit or seat or be in a settled position or place

examples: lay the books on the table
lay the patient carefully onto the bed

meanings.definition: cause to come to the ground
examples: the pilot managed to land the airplane safely

meanings.definition: reduce in worth or character, usually verbally
examples: She tends to put down younger women colleagues
His critics took him down after the lecture
drop off the passengers at the hotel
The customs agents destroyed the dog that was found to be rabid
the sick cat had to be put down

meanings.definition: put down in writing; of texts, musical compositions, etc.
make a record of; set down in permanent form
